steps.title: Extract data points and calculate slope
steps.content: From the table: at $t = 0$ (year 2000), population is 862; at $t = 10$ (year 2010), population is 846. The slope is $$\frac{846 - 862}{10 - 0} = \frac{-16}{10} = -1.6 \text{ people per year}$$

steps.title: Write the linear equation using point-slope form
steps.content: Since $t$ is measured in years after 2000, we start at the point $(0, 862)$. With slope $-1.6$, the equation is $$P(t) = 862 - 1.6t$$ Verify with the 2010 data: $P(10) = 862 - 1.6(10) = 862 - 16 = 846$ ✓ That matches choice A — A is correct.
